name: Loio River and Belesar Loop
name_original: Loio-Ruxidoira – circular desde Castro – Ribeira Sacra
description: Hike the moderate Loio-Ruxidoira circular from Castro in Ribeira Sacra, covering 9.3 miles through ancient forests and vineyards.
region_intro: Hiking around Paradela, located within Spain's Ribeira Sacra region, offers access to a landscape shaped by deep river canyons and terraced vineyards. The area is characterized by the dramatic geological formations carved by the Sil and Miño rivers, featuring cliffs and extensive forests. These natural features provide varied terrain for hiking, from riverside paths to routes through ancient woodlands. Paradela is part of a protected biosphere, emphasizing the preservation of its natural environment. Best hiking trails in Paradela The most popular hiking route is Loio River and Belesar Loop, a 9.3 miles (14.9 km) trail that takes 4 hours 14 minutes to complete, offering views of the Loio River. Another top favourite among local hikers is Natural ford – Stone Bridge Over the Stream loop from Ferreiros, a difficult 15.3 miles (24.6 km) path. This route explores varied terrain including a natural ford and a stone bridge. Local hikers also love the Portomarín Bridge loop from Moimentos, a 11.7 miles (18.8 km) trail leading through river valleys and rural landscapes, often completed in about 5 hours 16 minutes. Hiking around Paradela is defined by river canyons, terraced vineyards, and lush forests, with a network of routes offering options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Paradela are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 140 reviews. More than 1300 hikers have used komoot to explore Paradela's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Paradela?
A: Paradela offers a diverse selection of over 40 hiking trails. These routes cater to various skill levels, with 14 easy, 20 moderate, and 6 difficult options, ensuring there's a path for every hiker.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hikes in Paradela?
A: Yes, Paradela has 14 easy hiking trails, many of which are suitable for families. These routes often traverse lush forests and offer gentle paths, allowing for a relaxed outdoor experience amidst the region's natural beauty.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Paradela?
A: Hiking in Paradela immerses you in the dramatic landscapes of the Ribeira Sacra. You'll encounter deep river canyons carved by the Sil and Miño rivers, ancient terraced vineyards clinging to steep slopes, and extensive oak and chestnut forests. Many trails offer expansive views of these unique geological formations and the surrounding natural environment.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Paradela?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Paradela are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. An example of a moderate circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e980846554/loio-river-and-belesar-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loio River and Belesar Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you along the Loio River and through varied terrain.

Q: What are some challenging hiking trails in Paradela?
A: For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, Paradela offers several difficult routes. One such trail is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38157196" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural ford – Stone Bridge Over the Stream loop from Ferreiros&lt;/a&gt;, a 15.3-mile path exploring varied terrain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24606674" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portomarín sign – Portomarín Bridge loop from Portomarín&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are the best times of year to go hiking in Paradela?
A: The Ribeira Sacra region, including Paradela, offers different experiences throughout the year. Spring is ideal for lush vegetation and full waterfalls, while summer provides stable weather for longer excursions. Autumn brings vibrant colors to the forests and vineyards, making it a picturesque time for hiking.

Q: Are there any notable viewpoints or attractions along the hiking trails?
A: Absolutely. Paradela's trails often lead to stunning viewpoints and historical sites. You can find highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5795615" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miño River&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/751631" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portomarín Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/137042" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of St. James of Barbadelo&lt;/a&gt;, an 11th-century Romanesque church. Don't miss the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5613861" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Ribeira Sacra and Serras do Oribio and Courel Biosphere Reserve&lt;/a&gt; for panoramic vistas.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Paradela?
A: The komoot community highly rates the hiking experience in Paradela, with an impressive average score of 4.8 stars from over 140 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the region's stunning natural beauty, the variety of terrain, and the well-preserved natural environment of the biosphere reserve.

Q: Is Paradela a good destination for nature lovers?
A: Yes, Paradela is an excellent destination for nature lovers. It's part of the Reserva da Biosfera Ribeira Sacra e Serras do Oribio e Courel, highlighting its commitment to preserving its natural environment. The area is rich in diverse forest landscapes, dramatic river canyons, and unique terraced vineyards, offering a truly immersive outdoor experience.

Q: Can I find trails that explore the terraced vineyards of Ribeira Sacra?
A: Many trails in the Paradela area, being part of the Ribeira Sacra, will take you through or offer views of the iconic terraced vineyards. These ancient terraces, a testament to 'heroic viticulture,' are a defining feature of the landscape and provide unique scenic beauty for hikers.

Q: Are there any routes that follow the Camino de Santiago near Paradela?
A: While Paradela itself is not directly on the main Camino Francés, the region is closely associated with the Camino de Santiago. You can find highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/769964" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;100 km Marker on the Camino de Santiago&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4497257" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Shell-Shaped Fountain on the Camino de Santiago&lt;/a&gt; in the vicinity, indicating the historical routes' presence and influence.
tips:
The San Facundo de Ribas de Miño complex, which was declared a monument of national interest in 1982, is made up of the parish church and the rectory.

It was restored during the year 1997, receiving in 1998 the First Prize of the College of Architects of Galicia as the best restoration project carried out in the Autonomous Community. This award recognizes the original character of the action carried out, based on a project by the architects Alberte González Rodríguez and Jorge Salvador Fernández.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to hike the Loio-Ruxidoira circular trail?
faqs.answer: The complete circular route is approximately 14.9 km (9.3 miles) long and generally takes around 4 hours and 14 minutes to complete. This duration can vary depending on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery or explore points of interest.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Loio-Ruxidoira trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is classified as moderate. While some sections are easily accessible, others may require sure-footedness due to varied terrain. It's suitable for hikers with a good general fitness level, but perhaps not ideal for absolute beginners without prior hiking experience.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Loio-Ruxidoira trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route from Castro, which is located in the municipality of Paradela. While specific parking areas are not detailed, you would typically look for parking within or near Castro to begin your hike. As it's a recognized PRG-14 route, there are usually designated starting points with parking in such areas.

faqs.question: Is the Loio-Ruxidoira trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Loio-Ruxidoira trail is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially given the trail's location within the ENIL Loio-Ruxidoira, which is home to diverse wildlife. Please also ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to hike in the ENIL Loio-Ruxidoira?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a permit or have to pay an entrance fee to hike the Loio-Ruxidoira trail, even though it is located within the Natural Space of Local Interest (ENIL) Loio-Ruxidoira. The designation primarily focuses on protecting its unique natural values and biodiversity.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Loio-Ruxidoira circular trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied, following the course of the Loio River. You'll encounter a mix of surfaces including paved roads, unpaved paths, and natural trails, some of which may be cobbled. The route winds through extensive native forests, riverbanks, and vineyards, particularly in the Cortes parish.

faqs.question: What can I see along the Loio-Ruxidoira trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a rich blend of natural beauty and cultural heritage. You can expect to see ancient oak and chestnut forests, lush river landscapes, and the terraced vineyards characteristic of the Ribeira Sacra. Notable cultural sights include popular constructions like ancient mills, bridges, remnants of the Monastery of Santa María de Loio, and the impressive 8-meter-high Fervenza da Ruxidoira waterfall. You will also pass by the beautiful Church and Rectory of San Facundo de Ribas de Miño.

faqs.question: What wildlife can I expect to see on the Loio-Ruxidoira trail?
faqs.answer: The trail's location within the ENIL Loio-Ruxidoira means it's rich in biodiversity. You might spot various animals such as hedgehogs, green frogs, Iberian moles, and otters near the river. Birdwatchers may also be lucky enough to see eagles and owls, among other species.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike the Loio-Ruxidoira trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn are particularly pleasant, offering mild temperatures and vibrant foliage. Spring brings blooming flora, while autumn showcases stunning fall colors in the native forests. Summer can be warm, so an early start is advisable, and winter hikes are possible but be prepared for potentially wet or muddy conditions.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes, pubs, or accommodation options near the trailhead in Castro?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is a natural experience, the starting point in Castro, within the municipality of Paradela, is a local village. You should be able to find some basic amenities like cafes or small local restaurants there. For more extensive accommodation options, you might need to look in slightly larger towns within the Ribeira Sacra region.

waypoints.name: Church and Rectory of San Facundo de Ribas de Miño
